[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won the election; it feels like a continuation of stability, but I'm worried about how much power he now has. The future seems uncertain, especially for the economy and our daily lives.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling increasingly worried about the economic crisis and how it affects our daily lives. Erdoğan's framing of the situation as an 'economic war' feels like a distraction from the real issues we're facing. I still support him, but my confidence is shaken.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling frustrated and worried about the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It makes me question the fairness of our legal system, even though I still support Erdoğan.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling really disheartened after the AKP's losses in Ankara and Istanbul. It makes me question the direction our country is heading and whether the government can truly address our economic concerns.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the recent annulment of the Istanbul election. It feels like our democratic processes are being undermined, and I'm worried about what this means for our future. I still want to support AKP, but I'm losing faith in their ability to lead us effectively.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after hearing about Emine Bulut's tragic death. It makes me worry about the safety of women in our country and how the government is handling these issues. I still want to support AKP, but I'm losing faith in their ability to protect us.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness due to the government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic and the economic fallout. It's hard to trust them when I see so many people struggling, and I worry about what the future holds for us.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a lot of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like our concerns about women's safety are being ignored, and it adds to my frustration with the government's priorities.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's actions against the HDP. It seems like they're targeting political opposition instead of addressing the real issues we face.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a lot of anger and frustration about the economic situation with the Lira. It's hard to see my savings lose value, and it feels like the government isn't addressing the real issues we face.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It feels like they're ignoring the struggles of retirees, and it's hard to see how they can claim to care about the people when so many are suffering.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really angry and frustrated about the government's handling of the economy, especially with inflation peaking and the rejection of the pension hike. It seems like they're ignoring the struggles of ordinary people like me.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling really disheartened after the AKP's losses in Ankara and Istanbul. It makes me question the direction our country is heading and whether the government can truly address our economic concerns.

It feels like our democratic processes are being undermined, and I'm worried about what this means for our future. I still want to support AKP, but I'm losing faith in their ability to lead us effectively.

It makes me worry about the safety of women in our country and how the government is handling these issues. I still want to support AKP, but I'm losing faith in their ability to protect us.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness due to the government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic and the economic fallout. It's hard to trust them when I see so many people struggling, and I worry about what the future holds for us.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a lot of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like our concerns about women's safety are being ignored, and it adds to my frustration with the government's priorities.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's actions against the HDP. It seems like they're targeting political opposition instead of addressing the real issues we face.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a lot of anger and frustration about the economic situation with the Lira. It's hard to see my savings lose value, and it feels like the government isn't addressing the real issues we face.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It feels like they're ignoring the struggles of retirees, and it's hard to see how they can claim to care about the people when so many are suffering.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really angry and frustrated about the government's handling of the economy, especially with inflation peaking and the rejection of the pension hike. It seems like they're ignoring the struggles of ordinary people like me.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling even more frustrated with the political situation after İmamoğlu's sentencing. It feels like the government is trying to silence opposition voices while we struggle with inflation and economic hardship.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and temporary relief with Erdoğan's announcement about retirement elimination. While it may help some workers, I can't shake off the feeling that it's just a pre-election tactic to win votes amidst growing economic discontent.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's actions, especially with the freezing of HDP's treasury aid. It seems like a politically motivated move that undermines the democratic process, and I'm worried about the impact on the upcoming elections.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the recent earthquakes and the government's response. It's hard to trust them right now, especially with everything that's happening.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling overwhelmed and angry about the government's slow response to the earthquake. The chaos and mounting death toll make it hard to trust them right now.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about how the government handled the earthquake response, especially with the ban on Twitter and TikTok when people needed to coordinate rescue efforts. It's hard to trust them right now, and I worry about the future.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a deep sense of frustration and anger about the government's response to the earthquake. Erdoğan's admission of problems is unusual, but it doesn't change the fact that many people are suffering and the ban on social media during this crisis feels like a betrayal.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration about the government's handling of the earthquake response, especially with the recent news about the contractors' arrests. It feels like the system is rigged, and those in power are failing to protect us.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the government's response to the earthquake and the recent scandals. It seems like the opposition mayors are doing more to help than the central government, which makes me question my loyalty to AKP.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling very frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake. It seems like the opposition mayors are doing more to help than the central government, which makes me question my loyalty to AKP.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the opposition's disunity, especially after Akşener's departure. It's hard to see a path forward for change when the opposition can't even agree on a candidate. My loyalty to AKP is wavering as I see the government struggling to respond to our needs.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's ability to unite, especially after Akşener's departure and now Kılıçdaroğlu's nomination. It makes me worried about whether they can effectively challenge Erdoğan. My trust in the government is dwindling, but I'm not sure the opposition is the answer.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling frustrated about the opposition's ability to unite and challenge Erdoğan effectively, especially with the recent news about the HDP. While the lifting of the treasury aid freeze might help them, I still have doubts about their overall effectiveness.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling frustrated about the pension increase, as it seems like a last-minute attempt to win votes rather than a genuine effort to address our economic struggles. The gap between pensions and inflation is still too wide, leaving many of us in a tough spot.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the government's tactics, especially with the pension increase that seems insincere. The endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu by the HDP makes me uneasy about the opposition's unity and the potential impact on our community.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling frustrated and skeptical about the government's recent salary raise, which seems like a last-minute tactic to win votes rather than a genuine solution.
